title: Functions without parameters should not use (void)
description: Functions without parameters should not use "(void)"Analyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellQuick FixIDE quick fixes available with SonarQube for IDEconventioncppcoreguidelinesWhy is this an issue?More InfoExplicitly specifying avoidparameter list is required in C, but optional in C++. Usingvoidfor a parameter-less function decreases its readability. The at-a-glance impression is that the functiondoestake a parameter, and it takes a second look to ascertain that it does not.
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.
